claims.text: Alaska DNR issued a preliminary decision to transfer roughly 30 to 31 square miles of state land north of Houston to AIDEA.
claims.supporting_quote: Over 31 square miles of state-owned land north of Houston
claims.correction_note: Outlets differ: Mat-Su Sentinel reports 30 sq mi, Alaska's News Source over 31 sq mi. Use 30 to 31 range.

claims.text: The land transfer to AIDEA is a noncompetitive sale at no cost.
The proposal's stated purpose includes development of scalable data centers that leverage existing and planned utility corridors.
claims.supporting_quote: development of scalable data centers that leverage existing and planned utility corridors

claims.text: The DNR preliminary decision document is 41 pages.
The public comment period closes at 5 p.m. on August 19, 2026.
claims.correction_note: Mat-Su Sentinel corroborates the Aug 19 date; the 5 p.m. time comes from Alaska's News Source.

claims.text: The City of Houston has an ordinance to ban data centers, with the vote postponed to August 13, 2026.
claims.supporting_quote: Data center ban ordinance introduced June 2026; Vote postponed to August 13, 2026 meeting

claims.text: The Houston ordinance asserts data centers create a documented risk of accelerated permafrost thaw.
claims.supporting_quote: create a documented risk of accelerated permafrost thaw
claims.correction_note: Attribute to the ordinance/city, not as established fact.

claims.text: Alaska has 8 data centers, the fourth-fewest of any state, compared with 639 in Virginia.
claims.supporting_quote: Alaska: 8 data centers (fourth-fewest of any state); Virginia: 639 data centers (nation-leading)
claims.correction_note: Single-source; attribute to Alaska's News Source.

claims.text: AIDEA Executive Director Randy Ruaro said there are no pre-determined and set land use decisions made by AIDEA.
claims.supporting_quote: there are no pre-determined and set land use decisions made by AIDEA
claims.correction_note: Name, title, and verbatim quote confirmed.

claims.text: The site sits north of Houston, northeast of the Parks Highway, and near the Alaska Railroad, positioned as a freight logistics hub.
claims.value: north of Houston, near Parks Highway and Alaska Railroad
claims.supporting_quote: North of Houston, northeast of Parks Highway; Freight logistics hub near Alaska Railroad and Parks Highway
claims.correction_note: No verified road-mile distance from Anchorage; do not state a mileage.

claims.text: In March 2026 the Mat-Su Borough Assembly voted 6 to 1 to designate three parcels for development purposes, overriding a mayoral veto.
claims.supporting_quote: March 2026: Assembly voted 6-1 to designate three parcels for development purposes, overriding mayoral veto

claims.text: The proposed North Slope STAK data center project is estimated at over $10 billion.
claims.correction_note: Projection; hedge as reportedly or estimated.

claims.text: The STAK North Slope project would draw more than 1 gigawatt, about 30 percent more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.
claims.supporting_quote: some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid

claims.text: The STAK North Slope project spans about 1 square mile roughly 25 miles south of Deadhorse under a 50-year lease.
claims.value: ~1 sq mi, ~25 mi south of Deadhorse, 50-yr lease
claims.supporting_quote: Site Size: Approximately 1 square mile; Location: 25 miles south of Deadhorse; Lease Duration: 50 years

claims.text: The STAK project drew more than 500 public comments with fewer than a dozen endorsing it, ahead of a July 17, 2026 DNR comment deadline.
claims.supporting_quote: More than 500; Fewer than a dozen comments endorsed the project; Comment Deadline: July 17, 2026

claims.text: The Air Force identified about 4,700 acres across 12 parcels at JBER, Eielson Air Force Base, and Clear Space Force Station for potential AI data centers.
claims.supporting_quote: Approximately 4,700 acres across Air Force and Space Force installations; 12 parcels identified for potential development
claims.correction_note: Corroborated by af.mil. Background item, April 2026.

claims.text: The Air Force data-center leases would run up to 50 years.

dropped.text: Air Force Qualifying Project threshold >100 MW / $500M capex
dropped.reason: Not on either fetched Air Force source; RFLP-derived, unverifiable.

dropped.text: Terra Energy Center coal permit ~1,000 acres near Point MacKenzie for data-center tenants
dropped.reason: No fetched page supported it; Beacon source 403'd.

dropped.text: Specific road miles north of Anchorage; Port MacKenzie / West Susitna as this parcel
dropped.reason: Unverified; those references apply to separate borough project areas.

notes: Spine clears the bar: 11 verified spine claims across two independent in-window outlets (Mat-Su Sentinel 2026-07-15, Alaska's News Source 2026-07-16). Beacon and akleg 403'd (bot-block), not used. Acreage 30 to 31 (outlet spread). Attribute C7 (permafrost) to the ordinance, C8 counts to the outlet; hedge C12/C13 as projections; C16/C17 are April background.
